operation: First, create a 2D sketch on the XY workplane: start at the origin (0,0), draw a 20mm horizontal line to (20,0), add a three-point arc with endpoints (20,0) and (20,10) and midpoint (25,5) to form the convex rounded end, draw a 20mm horizontal line back to (0,10), then close the profile. Extrude this closed profile 5mm along the positive Z axis to form the base solid. Next, select the top maximum-Z face of the solid, create a workplane aligned to this face, sketch an 8mm × 6mm rectangle on this workplane, then perform a 3mm deep blind cut downward into the solid to form a rectangular recessed pocket.
geometry: This is a uniform-thickness prismatic solid, with one end formed as a convex curved edge and the opposite end as a flat straight edge connected by two parallel straight sides. A rectangular recessed pocket sits on the flat top face of the prism, extending partially through the total thickness of the part.
function: This is a small mechanical fitting intended for use as a spacer, slider insert, or mounting component, with the top recessed pocket designed to hold a mating fastener or matching part, and the curved end shaped to fit into corresponding slots for reliable alignment and secure assembly.
